name: Bayou La Batre Community Center
description: Please see the "Bayou Pickleball" Facebook page, or message the Facebook page. Other ways to verify the pickleball schedule are to call the Community Center at 251-824-4616 or check the calendar at www.cityofbayoulabatre.com Contracted use of the Gym for public and private events will sometimes alter the normal listed schedule.  The "regulars" who play are extremely friendly and welcome new players.
open_play_schedule: Make sure to check if the regular schedule is open or if changes have been made due to gym being rented.

name: Christ United Methodist Church
description: Christ United Methodist Church is a place to play pickleball in Mobile, AL. There are 6 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — $3.00 per day. No age limit.

name: Copeland-Cox Tennis Center
description: Copeland-Cox Tennis Center is a place to play pickleball in Mobile, AL. The court surface is hard. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Cottage Hill Baptist Church
description: Cottage Hill Baptist Church is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Mobile, AL. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Amenities include restrooms.

name: Crawford Park
description: Come play pickleball at Crawford Park in Mobile, AL! There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: Dumas Wesley Community Center
description: Dumas Wesley Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Mobile, AL. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like lights. A one-time fee is required to play. You can reserve the courts.
fees: Fee required — $3 for a day or $20 for 12 days

name: Hillsdale Community Center
description: Hillsdale Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Mobile, AL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Isle Dauphine Golf Club
description: Isle Dauphine Golf Club is a place to play pickleball in Dauphin Island, AL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: PickleRage - Mobile
description: Come play pickleball at PickleRage - Mobile in Mobile, AL! There are 12 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, locker rooms, food, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, lights, wheelchair accessibility, and water. You can reserve the courts.
fees: Fee required — PickleRage in Mobile, Alabama, offers various rates for open play and memberships, with non-member prices for 2-hour open play sessions ranging from $10 to $13 depending on the day and

fees:
Fee required — $12/hr/court – Non Peak – Monday – Friday (before 5pm)
$20/hr/court – Peak – Friday after 5pm, all day Saturday and Sunday, and selected holidays.

Business Hours
Sunday 11:00am-10:00pm
